scope: Chinese livestream-commerce and social-poster layout rules. These are operational defaults, not a universal visual style. Brand guidelines and explicit references override aesthetic defaults but never factual or readability gates.

story_or_video_cover.avoid_zones: Reserve interface-safe space near top and bottom according to the target platform.

wechat_longform.rule: Build repeatable vertical modules; do not shrink a feed poster into a long image.

rules: Never crop or obscure critical copy.
Give each critical block a controlled local contrast field.
Keep title, key fact, conversion anchor, and CTA readable in one three-second path.
Keep support copy readable at intended mobile size.
Reduce copy before reducing it below practical reading size.
Graphic layers may overlap or crop only when critical and support reading remain intact.
Every graphic layer must support hierarchy, grouping, navigation, atmosphere, or conversion.
Use one dominant title treatment per poster.
Use clear Chinese sans-serif type for dates, times, product names, prices, and rules unless the brand system requires another legible face.
Use decorative serif, handwriting, outline, metallic, or dimensional type only as a controlled display treatment.
For long Chinese titles, use two lines before reducing size; never horizontally compress glyphs.
Do not use random English words as texture. Microcopy must communicate real metadata.
Align repeated elements to shared edges and baselines.
Keep visible separation between Logo and primary title.
Use intentional asymmetry only after establishing an underlying grid.
Do not solve density by shrinking everything equally. Protect the primary hierarchy first.
Preserve recognizable product form, face, hands, garment features, packaging, and required accessories.
Normalize white balance, viewing angle, crop logic, and shadow direction inside a repeated set.
Use one authored crop with a clear subject role rather than treating photos as wallpaper.
Add a solid backing, local darkening, or a restrained mask under text on complex imagery.
Do not fabricate or silently replace a supplied person, product, Logo, or package.

aesthetic_priority: category, audience, campaign goal, and channel
palette_rule: Fallback palettes are starting points only. Use one main field, one text system, and one dominant accent unless the supplied brand requires more.
fact_and_price_rules: Preserve supplied identity, price, dates, rules, and qualifiers verbatim unless copy editing is explicitly requested.
Never invent benefits, coupons, gifts, accessories, endorsements, urgency, or missing text.
Separate instant discount, coupon value, rebate, red-envelope-after price, membership benefit, and conditional price.
Avoid unsupported absolute or lowest-price claims.
Use promotional symbol substitutions only when rewriting is authorized and the target-channel convention requires them.

production_contracts.visual_background: Generate composition, subject, lighting, materials, and reserved text fields without letters, numerals, Logos, watermarks, price figures, or fake interface text.
production_contracts.typesetting_specification: Place exact supplied Chinese copy, numbers, Logo, price mechanisms, rules, and disclaimers with deterministic tools when available.
